findings.message: Bath opens directly into LivingArea (Bath.right -> LivingArea.left). The bathroom door faces the main living/sleeping space with no buffer, which is a privacy violation in a studio.
Bath and Kitchen do not share a wall. Bath occupies (0,0)-(8,8) while Kitchen occupies (8,8)-(22,20); only their corners touch, forcing long plumbing runs between fixtures.
Kitchen has [top: solid, right: solid, bottom: solid, left: solid] - all four walls are solid with no window. The cooking area receives no natural daylight.
Front door is placed on Storage.bottom. Entering the unit drops you inside an isolated Storage room rather than a foyer or the main living area.
There is no clear path from the front door to LivingArea. The connection chain Storage -> Bath -> LivingArea forces residents to walk through the bathroom to reach the living area.
Storage has [top: solid, right: solid, bottom: solid, left: solid]. As an inhabited interior space (not a closet), it has no daylight.
Storage is laid out as a 96 sqft standalone room reached through Bath. In a 440 sqft studio, dedicating ~22% of the footprint to a hard-walled storage box is poor space economy.
Bath.left is a clear window on the building exterior with no privacy treatment noted. Bathroom windows should be frosted or otherwise screened.
